SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.absorber

Class SimpleAmineRegenerator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.util.NamedBaseClass
neqsim.process.SimulationBaseClass
neqsim.process.equipment.ProcessEquipmentBaseClass
neqsim.process.equipment.absorber.SimpleAmineRegenerator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Runnable, ProcessEquipmentInterface, ProcessElementInterface, SimulationInterface, NamedInterface
public class SimpleAmineRegenerator extends ProcessEquipmentBaseClass
Simple amine regenerator (stripper) model for solvent regeneration.

Models the thermal regeneration of a rich amine solvent that has picked up acid gas (CO2 and H2S) in an absorber. This is the counterpart of SimpleAmineAbsorber: it takes a rich amine inlet stream and produces a lean amine outlet stream (returned to the absorber) and an acid gas overhead stream (CO2/H2S sent to compression or vent).

The model uses a robust lumped acid-gas mass balance rather than a rigorous rate-based stripping calculation. The amount of acid gas stripped is governed by a target lean loading and a regeneration efficiency. The reboiler duty is estimated from three physically meaningful contributions:

  • Heat of desorption of CO2 from the amine (from AmineHeatOfAbsorption);
  • Sensible heat to raise the rich amine from its inlet temperature to the reboiler temperature;
  • Stripping steam latent heat needed to generate the stripping vapour.

Design Parameters

  • Lean loading target: residual mol acid gas per mol amine after regeneration (typically 0.01-0.20)
  • Reboiler temperature: typically 110-125 degC for MDEA, limited by amine degradation
  • Regeneration efficiency: fraction of the strippable acid gas actually removed
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim
See Also:

    • SimpleAmineRegenerator

      public SimpleAmineRegenerator(String name)
      Creates a simple amine regenerator.
      Parameters:
      name - equipment name

    • SimpleAmineRegenerator

      public SimpleAmineRegenerator(String name, StreamInterface richAmineStream)
      Creates a simple amine regenerator with a rich amine feed stream.
      Parameters:
      name - equipment name
      richAmineStream - rich amine inlet stream

  • Method Details

    • setRichAmineInStream

      public void setRichAmineInStream(StreamInterface stream)
      Sets the rich amine inlet stream.

      The lean amine and acid gas outlet streams are created as clones of the rich amine input.

      Parameters:
      stream - rich amine stream loaded with acid gas

    • getRichAmineInStream

      public StreamInterface getRichAmineInStream()
      Gets the rich amine inlet stream.
      Returns:

    • countAmineMoles

      private double countAmineMoles(SystemInterface system)
      Counts the total moles of molecular amine present in the system.
      Parameters:
      system - thermodynamic system to inspect
      Returns:
      total moles of amine (MDEA, DEA, MEA, AMDEA, Piperazine)

    • mapHeatType

      private AmineHeatOfAbsorption.AmineType mapHeatType()
      Maps the amine type string to a heat-of-absorption amine type.
      Returns:
      matching AmineHeatOfAbsorption.AmineType, defaulting to MDEA

    • calcReboilerDuty

      private void calcReboilerDuty(double co2StripMolesSystemBasis)
      Calculates the reboiler duty from the desorption, sensible and stripping contributions.
      Parameters:
      co2StripMolesSystemBasis - moles of CO2 stripped on the system-mole basis

    • getInletStreams

      public List<StreamInterface> getInletStreams()
      Returns all inlet streams connected to this equipment. Subclasses override to report their specific inlets. Used by graph builders, DEXPI export, and auto-instrumentation to discover topology without instanceof checks.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of inlet streams (empty by default)

    • getOutletStreams

      public List<StreamInterface> getOutletStreams()
      Returns all outlet streams produced by this equipment. Subclasses override to report their specific outlets. Used by graph builders, DEXPI export, and auto-instrumentation to discover topology without instanceof checks.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of outlet streams (empty by default)

    • run

      public void run(UUID id)
      In this method all thermodynamic and unit operations will be calculated in a steady state calculation.
      Parameters:
      id - UUID

    • getDesignSummary

      public String getDesignSummary()
      Gets a summary of the regenerator design and performance.
      Returns:
      design summary string
